observation.summary: Runtime upgrade flow evidence reports reserved rules, while self-improvement evidence requires the root loop open gap registry to stay closed.

hypothesis.summary: Keeping reserved runtime rules inside the runtime-upgrade evidence makes evidence debt observable without letting unresolved gaps count as closed self-improvement work.

execution.summary: Keep reserved runtime debt in runtime-upgrade evidence and register focused loop entries for concrete follow-up candidates.

evaluation.summary: loopevidence must report open_gap_count=0 while runtimeupgrade continues to report reserved rules separately.

retrospective.summary: Reserved rules stay valid executable knowledge, and concrete next-artifact debt is represented by focused loop entries rather than root open gaps.

evidence.proves: root loop evidence stays closed while runtime reserved debt is tracked in focused loop entries
runtime upgrade evidence still distinguishes reserved rules from implemented rules
